items.title: Procter And Gamble Reshapes Growth With Premium Baby Care And Health Push
items.description: Procter & Gamble (NYSE:PG) has expanded its premium Pampers line in China, achieving double digit growth in that segment despite weaker national birth trends. The company has acquired Wonderbelly, a health focused brand, as part of its broader push into wellness and over the counter style products. Together, these moves signal a wider repositioning of P&G toward higher value baby care and health related categories beyond its traditional staples. For investors watching NYSE:PG at a current...

items.title: Jim Cramer Says Procter & Gamble “Could Be a Huge Winner When It Reports Again Next Quarter”
items.description: The Procter & Gamble Company (NYSE:PG) is one of the stocks Jim Cramer put under the microscope. items.title: Procter & Gamble vs. Clorox: Which Household Name Is Worth Watching?
items.description: PG's scale and brand strength anchor stability, while CLX's focused execution, improving momentum and discounted valuation stand out amid near-term pressures.

items.title: Layoffs are piling up, heightening worker anxiety. Here are some of the biggest job cuts recently
items.description: As layoffs pile up, workers are feeling increasingly anxious about the job market.  Hiring has stagnated overall — with the country adding a meager 50,000 jobs last month, down from a revised figure of 56,000 in November.  Employers have initiated layoffs across sectors — with many pointing to rising operational costs that span from President Donald Trump’s barrage of new tariffs, stubborn inflation and shifts in spending from consumers, whose outlook on the U.S. economy recently plummeted to its lowest level since 2014.

items.title: Diaper Market Forecast and Company Analysis Report 2025-2033 Featuring Kao, Kimberly-Clark, P&G, SCA, Unicharm, Cott, Pigeon, Johnson & Johnson, Hengan International
items.description: The global diaper market is set to grow from $94.68 billion in 2025 to $160.63 billion by 2033, showcasing a CAGR of 6.83%. Rising birth rates in emerging markets and increasing demand for incontinence care drive growth in both baby and adult diaper segments. Urbanization, higher disposable incomes, and awareness of hygiene standards fuel consumer preference for convenient and high-quality diapers.
